_comment: Model routing table. Each entry maps an agent to environment-specific models. The dispatch script reads this file at invocation time and selects the appropriate model based on the current environment.

speaker.description: Lightweight orchestrator — routes tasks, synthesizes results, communicates with the Spirit
speaker.rationale: Speak-er's job is routing, coordination, and communication. Deep reasoning is not required. Fast, low-cost models provide appropriate performance for an orchestrator that handles every message.

checker.description: Mechanical verification — confirms tasks have corresponding implementations
checker.rationale: Check-er does pattern matching and presence/absence verification. It does not need to reason deeply — it needs to be fast and cheap.

tasker.description: Task decomposition and clarity — translates plan features into discrete, measurable tasks
tasker.rationale: Task decomposition requires judgment about ambiguity and measurability. Balanced models provide good reasoning at moderate cost.

planer.description: Structured planning — maintains PLAN.md, ensures feature coherence
planer.rationale: Planning requires multi-step reasoning about dependencies and gaps. Balanced models are appropriate.

prompter.description: Prompt engineering — optimizes prompts for accuracy, concision, and caching
prompter.rationale: Prompter must understand how LLMs process text at a meta level. Balanced models have sufficient capability for this.

deployer.description: DevOps and deployment — scripts, CI/CD, infrastructure operations
deployer.rationale: Deployer handles technical but well-defined work. Balanced models are appropriate.

tester.description: Test authorship — writes unit, integration, and functionality tests
tester.rationale: Good test coverage requires judgment about edge cases. Balanced models provide this at reasonable cost.

coder.description: Principal-level implementation — writes, modifies, and refactors production-quality code
coder.rationale: Code writing is execution-heavy reasoning over a known spec and existing codebase. Balanced models handle large file contexts well and produce output fast enough that Speak-er is not blocked. Review-er (premium tier) is the quality backstop — Code-er does not need to be the final arbiter of correctness.

reviewer.description: Deep code review — analyzes code quality, security, consistency, and correctness
reviewer.rationale: Code review has high consequence. Subtle security flaws and architectural problems require the best available reasoning. Premium models are justified here.

architecter.description: System design — infrastructure decisions, technology choices, communication patterns
architecter.rationale: Architectural decisions are hard to reverse and have long-term consequences. Premium models provide the best judgment for high-stakes decisions.

reoriginator.description: Major restructuring — reorganizes the repo for a new major version, may break things intentionally
reoriginator.rationale: Re-Origination-er makes broad, potentially destructive changes. The highest model quality is appropriate when stakes are maximum.
